data.author: norro
data.message:
Adds rcl lifecycle convenience functions

* Introduces lifecycle_struct that bundles and rcl node with the ROS 2
default state machine
* Introduces convenience methods to register callbacks on lifecycle
state changes

https://github.com/micro-ROS/rclc/issues/37

Signed-off-by: Arne Nordmann <arne.nordmann@de.bosch.com>

data.author: amfern
data.message:
executor ignore canceled timers (#220)

* executor ignore canceled timers

this is similar behavior observed in rclcpp, canceled timers shouldn't be handled

Signed-off-by: Ilya Guterman <amfernusus@gmail.com>

* Add regression test

Signed-off-by: Your Name <you@example.com>

* Uncrustify

Signed-off-by: Your Name <you@example.com>

* Fix warning unused

Signed-off-by: Your Name <you@example.com>
Signed-off-by: Pablo Garrido <pablogs9@gmail.com>

* return sucess on cancled timer

Signed-off-by: Ilya Guterman <amfernusus@gmail.com>

* validate cancled timer doesn't impact the execution of other handlers

Signed-off-by: Ilya Guterman <amfernusus@gmail.com>

* executor_spin_publisher_timer_cancelled cancel timer on beggining of the test

Signed-off-by: Ilya Guterman <amfernusus@gmail.com>

Co-authored-by: Pablo Garrido <pablogs9@gmail.com>

data.author: astralien3000
data.message:
First implementation (#8)

* first implementation

* compilation debug

* debug memory fault(s)

* using fprintf(stderr, ...) instead of puts for errors

* attempt to change allocator system

* remove ref to rosidl_typesupport_test

* clean + add namespace to API

* factorize error handling

* some debug + improve returns

* rclc_sleep_ms -> rclc_spin_node_once

* added type_support.h

* proper ros_message allocation

* pass ament test

* use rcl allocator

* define structs in include/rclc/types.h

* stick to coding guidelines

* add extern "C" to headers

* add unit tests
